<!--
  ADR TEMPLATE. Copy into docs/engineering/adrs/ADR-###-<slug>.md, then:
    1. Uncomment the frontmatter block (remove the outer <!-- --> wrapper).
    2. Assign the next ADR-### id. Never reuse a number.
    3. Delete guidance comments as you fill each section.

  ADRs are for BIG, costly-to-reverse decisions. The test: would undoing this be a
  PROJECT or an AFTERNOON? An afternoon goes in engineering/decision-log.md instead.
-->

<!--
---
id: ADR-###
type: adr
status: draft          # draft | active | locked | deprecated
phase: null
owner: james
tags: []
links: []
updated: YYYY-MM-DD
---
-->

# ADR-### — <Decision title>

**Status:** <draft | accepted | locked | deprecated>

## Context
<!-- What forces the decision? The requirement or constraint that made the old approach
     insufficient. Tag claims [intended] / [inferred] / [unknown] where relevant. -->

## Decision
<!-- What was decided, stated plainly. Cite evidence — commits, code, a written
     decision — rather than asserting intent from hindsight. -->

## Consequences
<!-- Split honestly. -->
### Good
<!-- What this buys. -->
### Costly / accepted
<!-- What it costs, and what complexity it forced that a simpler choice would not have. -->
### Unresolved
<!-- Open questions this decision leaves behind. [unknown] is a valid entry. -->
